root_text.sn56.62:1.1: Atha kho bhagavā parittaṁ nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsuṁ āropetvā bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn56.62:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yo vāyaṁ mayā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito, ayaṁ vā mahāpathavī”ti?
root_text.sn56.62:1.4: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ, yadidaṁ—mahāpathavī; appamattakāyaṁ bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito.
root_text.sn56.62:1.5: Saṅkhampi na upeti, upanidhampi na upeti, kalabhāgampi na upeti mahāpathaviṁ upanidhāya bhagavatā paritto nakhasikhāyaṁ paṁsu āropito”ti.
root_text.sn56.62:2.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, appamattakā te sattā ye majjhimesu janapadesu paccājāyanti; atha kho eteva bahutarā sattā ye paccantimesu janapadesu paccājāyanti aviññātāresu milakkhesu …pe….

translation_text.sn56.62:1.1: Then the Buddha, picking up a little bit of dirt on his fingernail, addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.62:1.3: Which is more: the little bit of dirt on my fingernail, or this great earth?”
translation_text.sn56.62:1.4: “Sir, the great earth is certainly more. The little bit of dirt on your fingernail is tiny.
translation_text.sn56.62:1.5: Compared to the great earth, it doesn’t count, there’s no comparison, it’s not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.62:2.1: “In the same way, the sentient beings reborn in central countries are few, while those reborn in the borderlands, among uneducated foreigners, are many. …”
